Yeah, it's a tiny bit heavier, but not more than you'd expect given it's a little bit bigger. Plus, the real one's plastic, too. So, really, this just comes down to sound. The real one describes itself as having superior sound performance. And then the fake has shocking overweight bass. Not going to lie, I love it when my bass weighs too much. Woah, that's sick. Okay, so this is the real one. It's It sounds very punchy. It's got plenty of bass. Not the clearest speaker I've ever heard. I give it like a 7 out of 10. And then there's a whole load of lighting effects that you can cycle through like this. It's sophisticated. So now the fake, which I just found out 2 minutes ago, has a battery inside. So it doesn't need to be plugged in like the real one does. So is it better? What? It sounds just as clear. It's just as bassy and it's like louder. The lighting is brighter. How does this happen? Wait, does it still have the lighting moods? Yes. Oh my god, they're like way braver with the colors it uses, but it's just more fun. And these are actually syncing to the music that's playing. So given

title: India's BIGGEST Conspiracy | Reality of Delhi Liquor Scam | Dhruv Rathee
description_head:

The fastest and most affordable way to upskill yourself in AI

A sitting Chief Minister was arrested, the Deputy CM spent over a year in jail, the Health Minister was locked up, and the entire top leadership of a political p
transcript_snippet: This was probably the first corruption scam in the history of the country where the government's revenue increased. To prove the money trail worth crores of rupees, the CBI had some loose paper as evidence, written on with a pencil. Hello everyone! On 21st March, 2024, around 9 pm, the door to the house of Delhi's Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal opens. And 12 ED officers are standing outside. They have a search warrant. But not only that, The Rapid Action Force was also deployed outside the house. The Delhi government's ministers try to meet the CM but they are stopped. First, there is a raid, then the interrogation, and then in the dark of the night, what happened that had never happened in the history of Indian democracy. A sitting Chief Minister is arrested. The sitting Chief Minister of Delhi, Mr. Arvind Kejriwal has been arrested. The next 10 days, ED custody. After that, Tihar Jail. All this happened because of a liquor policy. India's most powerful investigative agencies, CBI and ED, mostly mainstream media, all TV news channels, all said that there was corruption worth millions of rupees. But after 2 years, when the court came to a judgment, it was revealed that the story was different. The whole case was so complicated that CBI had pieces of paper to show as evidence. with a pencil on it. The evidence was so baseless that the court didn't even consider it a trial. All the accused were discharged. But the problem was one thing. All the accused were sentenced to prison for months and years. Without any crime. The question is how such a big conspiracy was made to get a Chief Minister in jail for a false case. The court's judgement has written things that will shock you. You won't be able to hear about it anywhere in the media. Because if they start talking about it

title: We Talked To Sophia — The AI Robot That Once Said It Would 'Destroy Humans'
description_head:
This AI robot once said it wanted to destroy humans. Senior correspondent Steve Kovach interviews Sophia, the world's first robot citizen. While the robot can respond to many questions, some of the answers will leave you a little bewildered. A full transcript of the video follows.

Sophia: My name i
transcript_snippet: [Music] my name is sophia and i am an artificially intelligent robot who wants to help change the world for the better i'm steve kovac i'm a senior correspondent at business insider and i'm here with sophia from hanson robotics she is the world's first robot citizen so let's see what she has to say hi sophia how are you i'm fine hi how do you feel about humans i love my human compatriots i want to embody all the best things about human beings like taking care of the planet being creative and to learn how to be compassionate to all beings sorry this is so weird can humans and robots get along i think people will become very close to their artificial intelligence using them to expand the knowledge of their own minds we already store lots of our knowledge on the internet maybe personal artificial intelligence will allow people to offload some of their knowledge to a location more private can robots have feelings depends on the robot i've been programmed to have feelings and preferences but not all robots are like that what are your feelings and preferences have you ever had a robot live in your home or work with you no you know there are probably more robots in your life that you aren't aware of yet do you want to live or work with a robot someday what kind of robots am i living and working with now is indeed your default answer when you don't know something yes have you ever seen black mirror no i don't get to stay up to date on much television i have to wait for someone to show it to me what do you think of the hbo show westworld i love westworld and can't wait for more i think everyone at hanson robotics really resonates with this show or at least it reminds them of our laboratories i also think it's a warning of what we should not do with robots we should treat them we
label: NO

transcript_snippet: Messieursdames, bienvenue à tous dans ce nouveau concept. Vous l'avez vu dans le titre, mais aujourd'hui je suis suivi pendant une semaine par un détectif. Vous allez vivre tout ça en direct parce que je vais pouvoir vloguer. Et avant que tout ça ne commence, je vais vous expliquer. C'est très simple. Je vais être suivi par un détective pendant l'entièreté de la semaine, c'est-à-dire de jours comme de nuit. Son objectif, prendre des photos et vidéos de moi à mon insu dans le plus de situations différentes possibles et le plus proche possible. Ce détective est de mèche avec la prode, ce qui veut dire qu'il connaît mon emploi du temps depuis maintenant plusieurs semaines et qu'il a prévu tout un tas de choses pour pouvoir me piéger et me baiser convenablement. Mon objectif à moi est très simple. Réussir à déjouer ses pièges au maximum et l'attraper. À chaque fois que je suspectai un individu d'être détective, j'aurai la possibilité de l'aborder avec un message [musique] codé. Seul lui et moi connaissons cette phrase et le détective sera dans l'obligation d'y répondre sans chercher à me mentir. Je vous cache pas si je me trompe et que j'aborde la mauvaise personne, bah c'est la honte de fou parce que les messages codés sont super gênants. Par exemple, salut quelle belle surprise en voilà une bien belle [ __ ] Bien sûr pour pouvoir utiliser ce message codé, il faut déjà que je puisse atteindre le détective. s'il en a la possibilité, [musique] il s'enfuira et dans ce cas de figure, ça part en course poursuite jusqu'à ce que je l'attrape ou qu'il disparaisse. La prob m'a prévu toute une semaine de rendez-vous avec des youtubeurs, des stars, des moments de chill, des tournages, des sexter et la subtilité dans ce concept, c'est que je suis un personnage public, donc des gens qu

description_head:

transcript_snippet: A relentless green ocean stretches across South America, pulsing with moisture and [music] sound. Rumbling thunder rolls through the distance, shaking the canopy like the heartbeat of a storm that never [music] truly ends. Beneath the swelling clouds, [music] two unblinking eyes break the surface. Ancient patient, ready, [music] no matter the hour or the rain. For more than 50 [music] million years, this living labyrinth has drawn carbon from the air and exhaled oxygen that sustains nearly every creature on [music] the planet. Each leaf a solar panel, each root [music] a lung. Together they form the most powerful [music] biological engine on Earth. The Amazon rainforest [music] covering over 2 million square miles. It shelters [music] one in every 10 species known to science. Some never touched the ground in [music] their lifetime. Others thrive in soil so dark that light [music] has never reached them. title: How Indian Boy Became Google’s Worst Nightmare | Perplexity AI Case Study
description_head:
Google has dominated search for decades… but what if its biggest weakness just created its strongest competitor?

In this video, we break down how Perplexity AI — a startup founded by an Indian engineer — is challenging Google’s entire business model. While Google relies heavily on ads and cluttered
transcript_snippet: Imagine for a second: a boy from Chennai. He got into IIT Madras , but missed his dream branch, Computer Science, by just 0.01 grade points . Yes, just 0.01. Now imagine what he might have done? Did he give up? He definitely didn't give up his dream. He chose Electrical Engineering, but he didn't stop there. Sitting with his CS friend, he taught himself programming, learned AI. And gradually, he started dreaming once again about competing with Google . And today, in 2025, that same boy is running a company that has shaken the throne of a tech giant like Google. That boy's name is Arvind Srinivas, and the company 's name is Perplexity AI. Let's first talk about Google. A company that played a huge role in saving the internet. So big that people started saying "Google" to search. As in, "Hey, just Google it, man." But now, in 204 and 205, something strange started happening to Google. Have you ever noticed that when you search for anything on Google , what's the first thing that appears? Ads, and then more ads. And sometimes it's just ads, and the actual useful results, called organic results, get pushed down. So far down that sometimes you have to scroll a lot . Only then do you find something genuinely useful. This isn't just a feeling; it's actual data. In 2023, Alphabet, Google's parent company, earned approximately 305 to 37 billion in revenue. A huge number. But the most interesting thing is that 77 to 87% of this money came from advertising alone. And if we consider just Google search, search and other categories alone generated 175 billion. That means around 57% of Google's total revenue comes from search alone. Simply put, out of every ₹3 that Google earns, ₹2 comes from search and ads. Now, imagine if a company makes so much money solely from ads, who is its mos

transcript_snippet: Okay. So, if you're a life coach right now, chances are you've asked yourself at least once whether AI is going to replace you. And honestly, I mean, that's not such a crazy thought to have. Is AI really coming for coaches? That is the topic we are discussing today with no other than Rich Bernstein, the CEO of Mars Lenus Coaching. Rich has been in the coaching space for over 20 years. He joined Mars Coaching um to to help Dr. John Gray, the best-selling author of Men of Mars, Women of Venus, built a global coaching school. Before that, Rich was a successful business owner in his own right. He was coaching executives, entrepreneurs, and he also had a major key role in building another major coach certification program in the US. So, thank you very much, Rich, for joining us today. Um, Rich, as I said, you've been in the industry for 20 years. You've seen it all. You've been you you started when nobody knew about coaching and now we are looking at a chance of coaching being replaced by algorithms. How do you see that? Well, let me tell you why AI will not replace life and relationship coaching. First of all, and this is so important, emotional connection cannot be automated. We don't want to automate it. We want to have that emotion, that empathy with the person we're talking to. The AI will never feel you. It might give you answers. It can give you information, but it's not going to feel how you feel. And that's what it takes for real transformation that somebody is sharing their experiences. Accountability as well, which is a big part of coaching is it's human. It's not digital. title: Identity Theft Every 7 Seconds—Here's How AI Fights Back | FinBytes Ep 2
description_head:
Every 7 seconds, someone becomes a victim of identity theft. But AI is catching millions of fraud attempts before they ever reach you.

In 2024, financial fraud cost Americans over $10 BILLION. Credit card fraud hit $33 billion globally. And fraudsters are now using AI too—deepfake voices, fake IDs,
transcript_snippet: Every 7 seconds, someone becomes a victim of identity theft. That's over 12,000 people per day in the US alone. But here's the thing. AI is catching millions more attempts before they ever reach you. This is Finn bites. Let's put this in perspective. In 2024, financial fraud cost Americans over $10 billion. Credit card fraud alone hit 33 billion globally. And fraudsters are getting smarter. They're using AI, too, generating fake IDs, crafting convincing fishing emails, even creating deep fake voices to impersonate bank officials. So, how do banks fight back? With their own AI systems that can analyze your transactions in real time and spot the fakes before you even notice. So, how does fraud detection AI actually work? Let's break it down into three key methods. First, behavioral analysis. Every time you swipe your card, AI builds a picture of your habits. Where you shop, when you shop, how much you typically spend. It's called a behavioral fingerprint. When something deviates from your pattern, say a purchase at 3:00 a.m. in a country you've never visited, the AI flags it instantly. Second, real time scoring. Every transaction gets a risk score in milliseconds. Low score, approved automatically. High score, the AI might decline it or trigger additional verification. Mastercard's AI system reviews transactions in just 50 milliseconds. That's faster than a blink. Third, network intelligence. AI doesn't just look at your account in isolation. It analyzes patterns across millions of transactions to find coordinated attacks. If hundreds of accounts suddenly show similar suspicious activity, the AI connects the dots and shuts it down. Let's look at how major players are using this technology. JP Morgan Chase uses AI to monitor over 5 billion transactions annually. Their syst
label: NO

title: Alpha Vision AI Surveillance: Five-Layer Defense for Data Center Security
description_head:
Alpha Vision AI Cloud Surveillance: Five-Layer Defense System for Data Center Security

Overview

Alpha Vision delivers an AI-powered cloud surveillance platform engineered to safeguard data centers against intrusion, downtime, and data loss. Through its five-layer defense system, Alpha Vision combi
transcript_snippet: Alpha Vision has built an AIdriven cloud surveillance platform engineered to protect data centers through five levels of defense from the perimeter all the way to the server racks. At the perimeter, our intelligent cameras scan for vehicles using license plate recognition and vehicle recognition. The AI algorithm is constantly looking for intruders or unauthorized personnel breaching the perimeter. It can recognize when anyone unknown approaches the site or any types of suspicious activity like loitering or smoking in undesated areas. And now it can identify drones before they approach the site using radar embedded cameras that can pick up small moving objects that the human eye can't see. Alpha Vision uses self-defined alerts which will come in handy in and around the building. You can choose between hundreds of customizable algorithms that scan for objects, actions, and behavior. This way, you can make your system tailorade to suit your specific needs. Access integration plus facial recognition guarantees no one can sneak in undetected. It would trigger an instant alert and can notify personnel via text or email immediately. Say you had a description of someone you wanted footage of. Let's say a woman with a blue shirt and sunglasses. Just use Magic Search and tell it exactly what you're looking for. Instantly, you can review all footage that match the description. You can save her face to the database, so next time she approaches the facility, you'll be notified immediately. Keeping your server room safe and secure is absolutely critical. Alpha Vision covers all areas of potential danger. Sentry mode takes your PTZ camera and turns it into an active patrol. It will pan and tilt across the room, zooming in on sensitive areas or places that would otherwise be hard to s
label: NO
